slug: 手语不是比划_它有语法有诗
title: 手语不是比划——它有语法有诗

outline.concept: Sign languages aren't universal pantomime — ASL and Chinese Sign Language are mutually unintelligible, as different as English and Mandarin. The intuition that deaf people everywhere 'just gesture the same way' is flat wrong.
outline.query: 手语 不同国家 聋人 翻译 比划 看不懂

outline.concept: Signs decompose into meaningless sub-units — handshape, location, movement, palm orientation, facial expression — that combine by strict rules, exactly like phonemes in speech. Change one feature and you've changed the word, just like swapping /b/ for /p/ turns 'bat' into 'pat.'
outline.query: 学手语 手型 打错 差一点 意思全变 手势细节

outline.concept: Sign languages encode grammar spatially, not sequentially. A signer places referent A on their left, B on their right, then moves a verb from A's location toward B — that directional movement IS subject-verb-object. Space does what word order does in speech.
outline.query: 用手指方向 代词 空间 指来指去 位置 代替语法

outline.concept: Sign language poetry exploits visual patterns: repeating handshapes works like alliteration, rhythmic movement creates meter, and a 'visual pun' layers same-handshape different-movement signs the way spoken poetry layers rhyme. The medium changes; the impulse to make art from structure doesn't.
outline.query: 手语诗 视觉 韵律 美感 表演 重复 手型
